title: phlox austromontana
intro: Phlox austromontana, also known as the Alpine Phlox, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of eastern Australia and New South Wales. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its essential oils. It has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its soothing and calming effects. Herbal preparations of Phlox austromontana can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
